        name: bash_code_execution
        input: {"command": "python3 -c \"\n# Current data (as of early March 2026)\naapl_price = 264.72\naapl_eps = 7.92\naapl_pe = round(aapl_price / aapl_eps, 2)\n\ngoogl_price = 306.52\ngoogl_eps = 10.91\ngoogl_pe = round(googl_price / googl_eps, 2)\n\nprint('===== AAPL vs GOOGL P/E Comparison =====')\nprint(f'AAPL  | Price: \\${aapl_price:.2f} | TTM EPS: \\${aapl_eps:.2f} | P/E Ratio: {aapl_pe}')\nprint(f'GOOGL | Price: \\${googl_price:.2f} | TTM EPS: \\${googl_eps:.2f} | P/E Ratio: {googl_pe}')\nprint()\ndiff = round(aapl_pe - googl_pe, 2)\npct_diff = round((aapl_pe - googl_pe) / googl_pe * 100, 2)\nprint(f'Difference: AAPL P/E is {diff} points ({pct_diff}%) HIGHER than GOOGL P/E')\nprint()\nif googl_pe < aapl_pe:\n    print(f'=> GOOGL has the BETTER (lower) P/E ratio at {googl_pe} vs AAPL at {aapl_pe}')\n    print(f'   Investors pay \\${googl_pe:.2f} per \\$1 of GOOGL earnings vs \\${aapl_pe:.2f} per \\$1 of AAPL earnings.')\nelse:\n    print(f'=> AAPL has the BETTER (lower) P/E ratio at {aapl_pe} vs GOOGL at {googl_pe}')\n\"\n"}
